subject: [MULTICLOUD] Bump multicloud-k8s image to 0.8.2
commit_message:
[MULTICLOUD] Bump multicloud-k8s image to 0.8.2

Update k8splugin with version based on Helm3 libraries

Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-1295
Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-1338
Issue-ID: MULTICLOUD-1348
Signed-off-by: Konrad Bańka <k.banka@samsung.com>
Change-Id: I793ccc0366fc061c5f0cb1e580e805de70b3d88a

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2
> 
> Is it really a bug fix? If no then what's the reason for backporting this to Honolulu?

Well, it actually is a bug fix.
This new image contains 2 changes fixing 3 (linked) bugs. As far as two of them are not that big (arguably), third one (1384) revealed quite important from my pov issue introduced in Honolulu release, that's fixed within this new img.

Patch Set 2:

> --------> basic_cnf tests
> basic_cnf: PASS (06:41)
So I think we can treat gating as successful? I'm not sure if any other tests should be relevant? Therefore it'd be blocked now only by master-branch merge and -2CR?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2
> 
> Is it really a bug fix? If no then what's the reason for backporting this to Honolulu?

All are bug fixes. 1348 is really blocking CNF deployments currently and pnf_macro use case that has revealed that in particular. 1338 blocks the deletion of resources in certain situations, especially when k8s operators are utilized. Move to helm3 libraries was necessary to fix also latest issues with templating of recent Helm packages. In 0.8.1 we utilize library 2.14 that supports older helm3 packages. We couldn't upgrade to newer 2.x version as it blocks deployment of helm3 packages at all (since 2.16 helm 3 packages are not supported). In consequence, to fix issues with helm2 packages templating and to not introduce regression for helm3 support in general we have to move to helm3 libs.

Patch Set 2:

Ok then I'll remove -2 as soon as we have a merge in the master
